statement: Signing scope rescoped from --global to --local on C:/ClarityOS_Code. global commit.gpgsign=unset; local commit.gpgsign=true; local gpg.format=ssh; local user.signingkey=C:/Users/chris/.ssh/id_ed25519_signing.pub; local gpg.ssh.allowedSignersFile=C:/Users/chris/.ssh/allowed_signers. Doctrine #97 case study #24b closed. Supersedes prior OPEN entry at line 8 of pre-append dispositions.jsonl.

Floor commit 8cfe17b violated its own .gitattributes eol=lf policy: dispositions.jsonl and anchors.jsonl committed as CRLF on Windows host despite policy. CT-2 substrate review at COW-1 v4 endorsement validated JSON+schema but not EOL bytes; sha256 match (4ce30ce0…36d5da CRLF) gave false confidence on byte-shape. Caught by ET-1.W verify-live pre-append. Normalized to LF in this commit: 9 prior lines re-normalized + 4 new lines appended LF-native. Floor sha256 superseded by LF-canonical hash. Logged as Doctrine #97 case study #26.

LF normalization pipeline in ET-1.W paired-commit build silently fail-opened on /tmp path mismatch: Python content-guard hit FileNotFoundError instead of aborting; bash tr/heredoc path did not yield LF; commit 5535db6 stored CRLF blob despite intent. Caught post-commit by ET-1.W verify-live (CR-lines=12 vs expected 0), recovered via git reset --soft 8cfe17b (unpushed, no remote impact). Redo built file as true LF via Python open(...,newline='') with cwd-relative temp path and hard gate (CR==0 AND lines==13 AND content-guard 1-9==floor). Logged as Doctrine #97 case study #27. D-error-26 statement now true in this commit (LF normalization actually occurred); floor sha256 4ce30ce0...36d5da superseded by LF-canonical hash captured post-commit.
evidence: Python build with newline='' + cwd-relative temp
hard gate: CR==0 AND lines==13 AND content-guard PASS

statement: D-error-26 over-claimed anchors.jsonl as CRLF. Verify-live shows only dispositions.jsonl was committed CRLF at floor 8cfe17b; anchors.jsonl was committed LF (CR-bytes=0, sha256 3b9822fd...652e5c, eol=lf). The anchors-CRLF assertion was an unverified extrapolation from the dispositions.jsonl finding and from ET-1.W flags that repeated it; anchors EOL was never independently checked until ET-1.W pre-push verify-live. Consequence: no anchors normalization was required and none was performed; the ledger was already fully LF-compliant after b4a1f09 (dispositions.jsonl 13 lines LF + anchors.jsonl 3 lines LF). D-error-26's dispositions.jsonl-CRLF half remains accurate; its anchors.jsonl half is corrected by this entry. Logged as Doctrine #97 case study #28.
evidence: CT-2 D-error-26 draft 2026-06-18 14:37 EDT (without anchors verify-live)
CT-2 D-error-28 candidate 2026-06-18 15:03 EDT (without anchors verify-live)
CT-2 FRAGO ET1-ANCHORS-LF-01 endorsement 2026-06-18 15:07 EDT (without anchors verify-live)

statement: ET-1.W ledger-append builder corrupted UTF-8 via Python subprocess.run(text=True), which on this Windows host decodes git output as cp1252: section-sign (U+00A7, x3) and ellipsis (U+2026, x1) in dispositions.jsonl lines 10 and 12 were misdecoded then re-encoded into mojibake (U+00A7 -> bytes C3 82 C2 A7; U+2026 -> bytes C3 A2 E2 82 AC C2 A6) in commit fbcb3af, which was committed AND pushed before detection. The content-guard compared the re-read against itself so it passed, and json.loads accepted the mojibake (valid UTF-8). Caught post-push by ET-1.W byte-identity diff vs parent b4a1f09. Recovered via git reset --soft b4a1f09 + byte-preserving rebuild (Python bytes I/O: capture_output without text=, open mode wb) + pre-commit gates (lines 1-13 byte-identical to b4a1f09, mojibake-count 0, section-count 3, ellipsis-count 1) + git push --force-with-lease (lease on fbcb3af) replacing the corrupt commit with e2cdf89. Only U+00A7 and U+2026 were affected; em-dash and quotes were not involved (contra the initial draft). Logged as Doctrine #97 case study #29.
evidence: fbcb3af lines 10/12 mojibake byte-count: C3 82 C2 A7 x3, C3 A2 E2 82 AC C2 A6 x1, clean U+00A7 x0, clean U+2026 x0
b4a1f09 (parent, clean): U+00A7 x3, U+2026 x1, mojibake x0
root cause: Python subprocess.run(text=True) cp1252-decoded git UTF-8 output on Windows
recovery commit e2cdf89: U+00A7 x3, U+2026 x1, mojibake x0, %G? G, lines 1-13 byte-identical to b4a1f09
git push --force-with-lease=ops/ledger-floor:fbcb3af -> e2cdf89 forced update; origin/main 196683c unchanged
